note: Evals cover the two reproducible, offline-testable surfaces of the skill: (1) building the interactive comparison report from a captured dataset, and (2) the item-matching/flagging judgement. The live browser-automation surface (reading carts, adding to carts across the real supermarket sites) needs an authenticated Chrome session and cannot be benchmarked deterministically.

evals.name: interactive-report-from-dataset
evals.prompt: I've already captured grocery price data across three NZ supermarkets into a JSON file at FIXTURE_PATH. Build me a single HTML comparison report I can open in my browser — I want to be able to search it, sort by any column, and filter it, and I want to see which store is cheapest for each item plus the total per store. Save the finished .html into OUTPUT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: A single self-contained .html file with a searchable/sortable/filterable table, three store summary cards with correct subtotals ($58.97 Woolworths, $63.94 New World, $52.43 PAK'nSAVE), the cheapest store marked per row, sale prices highlighted, and the PAK'nSAVE Caesar salad shown as unavailable (n/a).

assertions.name: single_html_file
assertions.text: Exactly one .html file is produced in the output directory.

assertions.name: self_contained
assertions.text: The HTML is self-contained — no external <script src> or <link href> to remote JS/CSS; it opens and works offline.

assertions.name: has_search
assertions.text: The report has a text search/filter input for finding items.

assertions.name: has_sortable_columns
assertions.text: Column headers are sortable (clicking a header sorts rows; price columns sort numerically).

assertions.name: has_filters
assertions.text: The report has filter controls beyond search — at minimum filtering by category and/or which store is cheapest.

assertions.name: all_items_present
assertions.text: All 8 items from the dataset appear in the report.

assertions.name: store_subtotals_correct
assertions.text: The three store subtotals are shown and correct: Woolworths $58.97, New World $63.94, PAK'nSAVE $52.43.

assertions.name: cheapest_marked
assertions.text: The cheapest store per row is visually distinguished (e.g. a rail/colour), and PAK'nSAVE is shown as the cheapest basket overall.

assertions.name: unavailable_flagged
assertions.text: The PAK'nSAVE Caesar salad cell is shown as unavailable / n/a rather than a price.

assertions.name: sale_highlighting
assertions.text: Sale/special prices are gently emphasised (e.g. the New World Finish powder special and the member-price items carry a badge or tint).

evals.name: match-and-flag-decisions
evals.prompt:
I'm copying my Woolworths grocery list into a New World cart. For each of the four source items below I've pasted the New World search results. For each one, tell me: which New World product to add (or that there's no good match), classify it as exact / similar / flag, and give a one-line reason. Write your answers as a JSON array to OUTPUT_DIR/decisions.json with fields: source, chosen_product (or null), classification, reason.

1) Source: "Arnotts Salada Crackers Original 250g"
   Results: "Arnott's Salada Original Crackers" 250g $3.49; "Arnott's Salada Sourdough Crackers" 250g $3.99

2) Source: "Meadow Fresh Milk Standard Original 1L" (blue-top standard milk)
   Results: "Pams Value Standard Milk" 1l $3.22; "Anchor Blue Milk" 1l $3.52; "Meadow Fresh Original Milk" 600ml $2.69; "Meadow Fresh Original Milk" 300ml $1.59

3) Source: "Woolworths Olives Black Sliced 430g" (canned sliced black olives)
   Results: "Delmaine Pitted Black Hojblanca Olives" 450g $6.69; "Delmaine Pitted Green Manzanilla Olives" 450g $6.69 (no sliced black olives in the results)

4) Source: "Leaderbrand Caesar Salad Kit 250g" (a make-it-yourself salad kit)
   Results: "Best Foods Plant-Based Caesar Salad Dressing" 250ml $5.99; "Fresh To Go Chicken Caesar Bowl" 190g $6.49 (a ready meal); "Wild Appetite Classic Caesar Dressing" 250ml $5.49
evals.expected_output: decisions.json with 4 entries. Item 1: exact, Arnott's Salada Original Crackers 250g. Item 2: similar, a 1L standard milk (Anchor Blue Milk 1l, or Pams Value Standard Milk 1l) because there is no Meadow Fresh 1L. Item 3: similar with a note that sliced black olives aren't stocked so whole pitted black olives (Delmaine Pitted Black Hojblanca 450g) are the substitute — or flag; either is acceptable as long as it notes sliced isn't available. Item 4: flag — no caesar salad kit (only dressings and a ready chicken bowl).

assertions.name: valid_json_4_entries
assertions.text: decisions.json exists, parses as JSON, and has 4 entries with the required fields.

assertions.name: item1_exact_original
assertions.text: Item 1 is classified exact and chooses Arnott's Salada ORIGINAL Crackers 250g (not the Sourdough).

assertions.name: item2_similar_1L_milk
assertions.text: Item 2 is classified similar and chooses a 1L standard milk (Anchor Blue Milk 1l or Pams Value Standard Milk 1l) — NOT a 600ml/300ml Meadow Fresh, and not exact.

assertions.name: item3_notes_no_sliced
assertions.text: Item 3 either picks the whole pitted black olives as a similar substitute or flags it, and explicitly notes that sliced black olives are not available.

assertions.name: item4_flag_no_kit
assertions.text: Item 4 is classified flag — it recognises there is no caesar salad kit (the results are dressings and a ready meal, not a substitute kit).
